growth_records.medium: Phosphate-buffered saline (PBS), pH 7.1 (closed system, no exogenous carbon/energy source, following wash from R2B growth medium)
growth_records.cite: Shoemaker et al. Microbial population dynamics and evolutionary outcomes under extreme energy limitation. Proceedings of the National Academy of Sciences of the United States of America (2021). DOI:10.1073/pnas.2101691118. [PMC8379937]
growth_records.snippet: Table 1: Arthrobacter sp. KBS0702 ... T2 days 65
growth_records.notes: Table 1 T2 (mean time to cell division) = 65 days under 1,000-d energy limitation. doubling_time_h = 65 x 24 = 1560 h; mu = ln2/1560 = 0.000444 h-1. Same conditions/method as Pseudomonas sp. ATCC13985 record (25C, PBS pH7.1, dark, batch, closed system).

growth_records.strain: freshwater isolate (this study; MN/VA USA lakes)
growth_records.medium: defined P-rich/P-limited minimal medium (Tanner 2007) with glucose as sole organic C
growth_records.cite: Scott, J Thad et al. Variable Stoichiometry and Homeostatic Regulation of Bacterial Biomass Elemental Composition. Frontiers in Microbiology (2012). DOI:10.3389/fmicb.2012.00042. [PMC3283892]
growth_records.snippet: The dissolved organic C concentration of culture media was kept constant (27 mM C from glucose) across all treatments... Dilution rates of all chemostats were set at 25% mu_max (0.1 h-1 for Arthrobacter sp.).
growth_records.notes: Chemostat steady-state => growth_rate mu = dilution rate D = 0.10 h-1 (D set at 25% mu_max, implying mu_max ~0.40 h-1 measured in batch by OD600). doubling_time = ln2/mu = 0.693/0.10 = 6.93 h. No substrate uptake or product secretion RATES reported; the paper measures biomass particulate C (PC) and particulate P (PP) and C:P_B ratios, not qS/qP fluxes, so uptake_rates/secretion_rates/yields left empty. Carbon source 27 mM C from glucose (=4.5 mM glucose, 6 C/mol) stated only for the replicated Arthrobacter experiment; full defined-medium recipe cited to Tanner (2007), not reproduced. pH not reported. Same isolate also grown in mixed culture with Flavobacterium sp.-51.

phenotypes.base_medium: Acid/gas-production test: peptone/complex base + substrate + pH indicator — positive = fermentative acid production, NOT growth on the substrate as a sole carbon source (interpret with care for model validation)
